name: Scroll

unions.name: Axis
unions.comment:
Axis configuration for animation movement direction.

Use this to control whether your animation moves horizontally or vertically:

  - `Y` - Vertical scrolling (most common, default for page scrolling)
  - `X` - Horizontal scrolling (for sideways carousels or horizontal content)
  - `Both` - Both horizontal and vertical scrolling to reach the target element

unions.name: Container
unions.comment:
Type for configuring which element to scroll within.

Use `DocumentBody` for scrolling the main document, or `Container elementId`
for scrolling within a specific container element.

unions.name: Timing
unions.comment:
Animation timing configuration.

Choose between speed-based or duration-based timing:

  - Speed: Animation speed in pixels per second (higher = faster)
  - Duration: Animation duration in milliseconds (higher = slower)

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment:
Configuration for scrolling.

  - **timing**: Animation timing (Speed in pixels per second or Duration in milliseconds). Default is `Duration 400`.
  - **offsetX**: Horizontal offset in pixels from the target position. Default is 0.
  - **offsetY**: Vertical offset in pixels from the target position. Default is 12.
  - **easing**: Easing function from [elm-community/easing-functions](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-community/easing-functions/latest/). Default is [Ease.outQuint](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-community/easing-functions/latest/Ease#outQuint).
  - **axis**: Movement axis (Y for vertical, X for horizontal, Both for diagonal). Default is Y.
aliases.type: { timing : Scroll.Timing, offsetX : Basics.Int, offsetY : Basics.Int, easing : Ease.Easing, axis : Scroll.Axis }

aliases.name: ElementId
aliases.comment: Type alias for DOM element IDs.

values.name: defaultConfig
values.comment:
The default configuration which you can customize as needed.

    import Ease
    import SmoothMoveScroll exposing (Axis(..), Timing(..), defaultConfig)

    customConfig =
        { defaultConfig
            | timing = Duration 500
            , offsetY = 20
            , easing = Ease.inOutCubic
            , axis = Both
        }

name: Scroll.Cmd

values.name: jump
values.comment:
Instantly jump to an element without animation.

Perfect for immediate navigation where you don't want smooth scrolling.

    JumpToSection sectionId ->
        ( model
        , jump sectionId DocumentBody JumpComplete
        )

values.name: jumpToBottom
values.comment:
Instantly jump to the bottom of the document body or a container element.

Perfect for instant "jump to bottom" functionality without animation.

    JumpToBottom ->
        ( model, jumpToBottom (Container "chat-window") JumpComplete )

For document body jumping:

    JumpToBottom ->
        ( model, jumpToBottom DocumentBody JumpComplete )

values.name: jumpToBottomWithConfig
values.comment:
Jump to the bottom of a container with configuration (respects offset settings).

    JumpToBottom ->
        ( model, jumpToBottomWithConfig (Container "chat-window") JumpComplete { defaultConfig | offsetY = -10 } )

values.name: jumpToLeftEdge
values.comment:
Instantly jump to the left edge of the document body or a container element.

Perfect for resetting horizontal position without animation.

    JumpToLeft ->
        ( model, jumpToLeftEdge (Container "horizontal-scroller") JumpComplete )

For document body jumping:

    JumpToLeft ->
        ( model, jumpToLeftEdge DocumentBody JumpComplete )

values.name: jumpToLeftEdgeWithConfig
values.comment:
Jump to the left edge of a container with configuration (respects offset settings).

    JumpToLeft ->
        ( model, jumpToLeftEdgeWithConfig (Container "carousel") JumpComplete { defaultConfig | offsetX = 5 } )

values.name: jumpToRightEdge
values.comment:
Instantly jump to the right edge of the document body or a container element.

Perfect for jumping to end of horizontal content without animation.

    JumpToRight ->
        ( model, jumpToRightEdge (Container "horizontal-scroller") JumpComplete )

For document body jumping:

    JumpToRight ->
        ( model, jumpToRightEdge DocumentBody JumpComplete )

values.name: jumpToRightEdgeWithConfig
values.comment:
Jump to the right edge of a container with configuration (respects offset settings).

    JumpToRight ->
        ( model, jumpToRightEdgeWithConfig (Container "carousel") JumpComplete { defaultConfig | offsetX = -5 } )

values.name: jumpToTop
values.comment:
Instantly jump to the top of the document body or a container element.

Perfect for instant "back to top" functionality without animation.

    JumpToTop ->
        ( model, jumpToTop (Container "main-content") JumpComplete )

For document body jumping:

    JumpToTop ->
        ( model, jumpToTop DocumentBody JumpComplete )

values.name: jumpToTopWithConfig
values.comment:
Jump to the top of a container with configuration (respects offset settings).

    JumpToTop ->
        ( model, jumpToTopWithConfig (Container "main-content") JumpComplete { defaultConfig | offsetY = 10 } )

values.name: jumpWithConfig
values.type: Scroll.ElementId -> Scroll.Container -> msg -> Scroll.Config -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: scroll
values.comment:
Smooth scroll to a specific DOM element.

    ScrollTo elementId ->
        ( model
        , scroll elementId DocumentBody ScrollComplete
        )

values.name: scrollToBottom
values.comment:
Smooth scroll to the bottom of the document body or a container element.

Perfect for "scroll to bottom" functionality in chat applications or content sections.

    ScrollToBottom ->
        ( model
        , scrollToBottom (Container "chat-window") ScrollComplete
        )

For document body scrolling:

    ScrollToBottom ->
        ( model, scrollToBottom DocumentBody ScrollComplete )

values.name: scrollToBottomWithConfig
scrollToLeftEdge
values.comment:
Smooth scroll to the left edge of the document body or a container element.

Perfect for resetting horizontal scroll position or navigation.

    ScrollToLeft ->
        ( model, scrollToLeftEdge (Container "horizontal-scroller") ScrollComplete )

For document body scrolling:

    ScrollToLeft ->
        ( model, scrollToLeftEdge DocumentBody ScrollComplete )

values.name: scrollToLeftEdgeWithConfig
values.comment:
Scroll to the left edge of a container with custom configuration.

    ScrollToLeft ->
        ( model, scrollToLeftEdgeWithConfig (Container "carousel") ScrollComplete { defaultConfig | timing = Duration 300 } )

values.name: scrollToRightEdge
values.comment:
Smooth scroll to the right edge of the document body or a container element.

Perfect for horizontal navigation to the end of content.

    ScrollToRight ->
        ( model, scrollToRightEdge (Container "horizontal-scroller") ScrollComplete )

For document body scrolling:

    ScrollToRight ->
        ( model, scrollToRightEdge DocumentBody ScrollComplete )

values.name: scrollToRightEdgeWithConfig
values.comment:
Scroll to the right edge of a container with custom configuration.

    ScrollToRight ->
        ( model, scrollToRightEdgeWithConfig (Container "carousel") ScrollComplete { defaultConfig | timing = Duration 300 } )

values.name: scrollToTop
values.comment:
Smooth scroll to the top of the document body or a container element.

Perfect for "back to top" buttons or resetting scroll position.

    BackToTop ->
        ( model
        , scrollToTop (Container "main-content") ScrollComplete
        )

For document body scrolling:

    BackToTop ->
        ( model, scrollToTop DocumentBody ScrollComplete )

values.name: scrollToTopWithConfig
scrollWithConfig
values.type: Scroll.ElementId -> Scroll.Container -> msg -> Scroll.Config -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

name: Scroll.Container.Cmd

values.name: jump
values.comment:
Instantly jump to a DOM element within a container using default configuration.

    jump "container-id" "my-element" NoOp

values.name: jumpToBottom
values.comment:
Instantly jump to the bottom of a container using default configuration.

    jumpToBottom "container-id" NoOp

values.name: jumpToBottomWithConfig
values.comment:
Instantly jump to the bottom of a container with custom configuration.

    jumpToBottomWithConfig "container-id"
        NoOp
        { defaultConfig | offsetY = 20 }

values.name: jumpToLeftEdge
values.comment:
Instantly jump to the left edge of a container using default configuration.

    jumpToLeftEdge "container-id" NoOp

values.name: jumpToLeftEdgeWithConfig
values.comment:
Instantly jump to the left edge of a container with custom configuration.

    jumpToLeftEdgeWithConfig "container-id"
        NoOp
        { defaultConfig | offsetX = 5 }

values.name: jumpToRightEdge
values.comment:
Instantly jump to the right edge of a container using default configuration.

    jumpToRightEdge "container-id" NoOp

values.name: jumpToRightEdgeWithConfig
values.comment:
Instantly jump to the right edge of a container with custom configuration.

    jumpToRightEdgeWithConfig "container-id"
        NoOp
        { defaultConfig | offsetX = 15 }

values.name: jumpToTop
values.comment:
Instantly jump to the top of a container using default configuration.

    jumpToTop "container-id" NoOp

values.name: jumpToTopWithConfig
values.comment:
Instantly jump to the top of a container with custom configuration.

    jumpToTopWithConfig "container-id"
        NoOp
        { defaultConfig | offsetY = 10 }

values.name: jumpWithConfig
values.comment:
Instantly jump to a DOM element within a container with custom configuration.

    jumpWithConfig "container-id"
        "my-element"
        NoOp
        { defaultConfig | offsetY = 50 }
values.type: Scroll.ElementId -> Scroll.ElementId -> msg -> Scroll.Config -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: scroll
values.comment:
Smoothly scroll to a DOM element within a specific container using default configuration.

    scroll "container-id" "my-element" NoOp

This scrolls to the element with ID "my-element" within the container with ID "container-id".

values.name: scrollToBottom
values.comment:
Smoothly scroll to the bottom of a container using default configuration.

    scrollToBottom "container-id" NoOp

values.name: scrollToBottomWithConfig
values.comment:
Smoothly scroll to the bottom of a container with custom configuration.

    scrollToBottomWithConfig "container-id"
        NoOp
        { defaultConfig | timing = Speed 800 }

values.name: scrollToLeftEdge
values.comment:
Smoothly scroll to the left edge of a container using default configuration.

    scrollToLeftEdge "container-id" NoOp

values.name: scrollToLeftEdgeWithConfig
values.comment:
Smoothly scroll to the left edge of a container with custom configuration.

    scrollToLeftEdgeWithConfig "container-id"
        NoOp
        { defaultConfig | timing = Duration 400 }

values.name: scrollToRightEdge
values.comment:
Smoothly scroll to the right edge of a container using default configuration.

    scrollToRightEdge "container-id" NoOp

values.name: scrollToRightEdgeWithConfig
values.comment:
Smoothly scroll to the right edge of a container with custom configuration.

    scrollToRightEdgeWithConfig "container-id"
        NoOp
        { defaultConfig | timing = Duration 500 }

values.name: scrollToTop
values.comment:
Smoothly scroll to the top of a container using default configuration.

    scrollToTop "container-id" NoOp

values.name: scrollToTopWithConfig
values.comment:
Smoothly scroll to the top of a container with custom configuration.

    scrollToTopWithConfig "container-id"
        NoOp
        { defaultConfig | timing = Duration 600 }

values.name: scrollWithConfig
values.comment:
Smoothly scroll to a DOM element within a container with custom configuration.

    scrollWithConfig "container-id"
        "my-element"
        NoOp
        { defaultConfig
            | axis = X
            , offsetX = 20
        }
values.type: Scroll.ElementId -> Scroll.ElementId -> msg -> Scroll.Config -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

name: Scroll.Task

values.name: jump
values.comment:
Task-based instant jumping for advanced users who need error handling.

    JumpToSection sectionId ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleJumpError <|
            jump sectionId DocumentBody
        )

values.name: jumpToBottom
values.comment:
Task-based instant jumping to bottom for advanced users who need error handling.

    JumpToBottom ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleJumpError <|
            jumpToBottom DocumentBody
        )

values.name: jumpToBottomWithConfig
values.comment:
Task-based jumping to bottom with custom configuration.

    JumpToBottom ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleJumpError <|
            jumpToBottomWithConfig (Container "main-content") <|
                { defaultConfig | offsetY = -10 }
        )

values.name: jumpToLeftEdge
values.comment:
Task-based instant jumping to left edge for advanced users who need error handling.

    JumpToLeft ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleJumpError <|
            jumpToLeftEdge DocumentBody
        )

values.name: jumpToLeftEdgeWithConfig
values.comment:
Task-based jumping to left edge with custom configuration.

    JumpToLeft ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleJumpError <|
            jumpToLeftEdgeWithConfig (Container "carousel") <|
                { defaultConfig | offsetX = 5 }
        )

values.name: jumpToRightEdge
values.comment:
Task-based instant jumping to right edge for advanced users who need error handling.

    JumpToRight ->

        , Task.attempt HandleJumpError <|

Task-based jumping to right edge with custom configuration.

    JumpToRight ->

values.name: jumpToTop

    JumpToTop ->

        )

values.name: jumpWithConfig
values.comment:
Task-based instant jumping with configuration for advanced users who need error handling.

    JumpToSection sectionId ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleJumpError <|
            jumpWithConfig sectionId DocumentBody <|
                { defaultConfig | offsetY = 50 }
        )

values.name: scroll
values.comment:
Task-based scrolling to an element for advanced users who need error handling.

    ScrollTo elementId ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleScrollError <|
            scroll elementId DocumentBody
        )

values.name: scrollToBottom
values.comment:
Task-based scrolling to bottom for advanced users who need error handling.

    ScrollToBottom ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleScrollError <|
            scrollToBottom DocumentBody
        )

Task-based scrolling to bottom with custom configuration.

    ScrollToBottom ->

values.name: scrollToLeftEdge
values.comment:
Task-based scrolling to left edge for advanced users who need error handling.

    ScrollToLeft ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleScrollError <|
            scrollToLeftEdge DocumentBody
        )

values.name: scrollToLeftEdgeWithConfig
values.comment:
Task-based scrolling to left edge with custom configuration.

    ScrollToLeft ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleScrollError <|
            scrollToLeftEdgeWithConfig (Container "carousel") <|

values.name: scrollToRightEdge
values.comment:
Task-based scrolling to right edge for advanced users who need error handling.

    ScrollToRight ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleScrollError <|
            scrollToRightEdge DocumentBody

values.name: scrollToRightEdgeWithConfig

Task-based scrolling to right edge with custom configuration.

    ScrollToRight ->

values.name: scrollToTop
values.comment:
Task-based scrolling to top for advanced users who need error handling.

    BackToTop ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleScrollError <|
            scrollToTop DocumentBody
        )

values.name: scrollToTopWithConfig
values.comment:
Task-based scrolling to top with custom configuration.

    BackToTop ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleScrollError <|
            scrollToTopWithConfig (Container "main-content") <|
                { defaultConfig | timing = Duration 500 }
        )

values.name: scrollWithConfig
values.comment:
Task-based scrolling with custom configuration.

    ScrollTo elementId ->
        ( model
        , Task.attempt HandleScrollError <|
            scrollWithConfig elementId container config
        )

name: SmoothMoveCSS

unions.name: Model
unions.comment:
Model for tracking multiple element positions

Uses a Dict for O(1) position lookups and efficient management of many elements.

unions.name: Timing
unions.comment:
Animation timing configuration

Choose between speed-based or duration-based timing:

  - Speed: Animation speed in pixels per second (higher = faster)
  - Duration: Animation duration in milliseconds (higher = slower)

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment:
Configuration for CSS-based animations

  - timing: Animation timing (Speed in pixels per second or Duration in milliseconds)
  - easing: CSS easing function ("ease-out", "cubic-bezier(0.4, 0.0, 0.2, 1)", etc.)

aliases.name: Position
aliases.comment: Position type alias for X and Y coordinates

values.name: animateTo
values.comment:
Update an element's position for CSS animation

This function updates the model state. Apply the position in your view with `transform`:

    -- In update
    AnimateBox ->
        { model | animations = animateTo "box" 300 200 model.animations }

    -- In view
    div
        [ style "transform" (transform "box" model.animations)
        , style "transition" (transition defaultConfig)
        ]
        [ text "Animated box" ]

values.name: animateToX
animateToY
calculateDuration
values.comment:
Calculate animation duration in milliseconds based on distance and timing config

Useful for coordinating animations or knowing when transitions will complete.


    duration =
        calculateDuration defaultConfig ( 100, 200 ) ( 300, 150 )

    -- Returns duration in milliseconds for moving from (100,200) to (300,150)
values.type: SmoothMoveCSS.Config -> ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) -> ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) -> Basics.Float

values.name: defaultConfig
values.comment: Default configuration with smooth easing

values.name: getPosition
values.comment:
Get the current position of an element

Returns `Nothing` if the element hasn't been positioned yet:

    case getPosition "box" model.animations of
        Just pos ->
            text ("Box is at " ++ String.fromFloat pos.x ++ ", " ++ String.fromFloat pos.y)

        Nothing ->
            text "Box position not set"

values.name: init
values.comment:
Initialize an empty model with no element positions

    initialModel =
        init

values.name: onTransitionCancel
values.comment:
Listen for when a CSS transition is cancelled

This happens when a transition is interrupted before completion,
such as when the element is removed or another transition starts.

    div
        [ style "transform" (SmoothMoveCSS.transform x y)
        , style "transition" (SmoothMoveCSS.transition SmoothMoveCSS.defaultConfig)
        , SmoothMoveCSS.onTransitionCancel TransitionCancelled
        ]
        [ text "Animated element" ]

values.name: onTransitionEnd
values.comment:
Listen for when a CSS transition completes

    div
        [ style "transform" (SmoothMoveCSS.transform x y)
        , style "transition" (SmoothMoveCSS.transition SmoothMoveCSS.defaultConfig)
        , SmoothMoveCSS.onTransitionEnd TransitionCompleted
        ]
        [ text "Animated element" ]

This is the most commonly used transition event for coordinating animations
or updating UI state when animations finish.

values.name: onTransitionRun
values.comment:
Listen for when a CSS transition is created (even if delayed)

This event fires when a transition is created, even if it has a delay.
It fires before transitionstart.

    div
        [ style "transform" (SmoothMoveCSS.transform x y)
        , style "transition" (SmoothMoveCSS.transition SmoothMoveCSS.defaultConfig)
        , SmoothMoveCSS.onTransitionRun TransitionCreated
        ]
        [ text "Animated element" ]

values.name: onTransitionStart
values.comment:
Listen for when a CSS transition starts

    div
        [ style "transform" (SmoothMoveCSS.transform x y)
        , style "transition" (SmoothMoveCSS.transition SmoothMoveCSS.defaultConfig)
        , SmoothMoveCSS.onTransitionStart TransitionStarted
        ]
        [ text "Animated element" ]

values.name: setPosition
values.comment:
Set the position for an element

If not set, the element defaults to (0,0):

    model
        |> setPosition "box1" 100 150
        |> setPosition "box2" 200 250

values.name: transform
values.comment:
Create a CSS transform string for positioning

Use this when you are managing positions manually

    div
        [ style "transform" <|
            transform 100 200
        ]
        [ text "Positioned at (100, 200)" ]

values.name: transformElement
values.comment:
Generate CSS transform string for a specific element

Use this when SmoothMoveCSS is managing element(s) positions

    div
        [ style "transform" (transformElement "box" model.animations)
        , style "transition" (transition defaultConfig)
        ]
        [ text "Box" ]

values.name: transformPosition
values.comment:
Create a CSS transform string from a Position record

    let
        pos =
            { x = 150, y = 250 }
    in
    div
        [ style "transform" <|
            transformPosition pos
        ]
        [ text "Positioned at (150, 250)" ]

values.name: transition
values.comment:
Creates a transition for the transform property with your configuration.
For default configuration, pass `defaultConfig`.

    div
        [ style "transform" <|
            transform targetX targetY
        , style "transition" <|
            transition defaultConfig
        ]
        [ text "Animated element" ]

    -- Or with custom config
    customConfig =
        { defaultConfig | timing = Speed 200 }

    div
        [ style "transform" <|
            transform targetX targetY
        , style "transition" <|
            transition customConfig
        ]
        [ text "Custom animated element" ]

values.name: transitionWithDistance
values.comment:
Generate CSS transition property with distance-based duration

Calculates duration based on the distance traveled and timing configuration.
Use this when you want the animation speed to feel consistent regardless of distance.

    let
        distance =
            sqrt ((targetX - currentX) ^ 2 + (targetY - currentY) ^ 2)
    in
    div
        [ style "transform" (SmoothMoveCSS.transform targetX targetY)
        , style "transition" (SmoothMoveCSS.transitionWithDistance distance defaultConfig)
        ]
        [ text "Animated element" ]

values.name: transitionWithSpeed
values.comment:
Generate CSS transition property with speed-based timing

This is a convenience function for creating transitions with Speed-based timing.
Calculates the duration based on the distance and speed (pixels per second).

    let
        -- pixels to travel
        distance =
            100

        -- pixels per second
        speed =
            200
    in
    div
        [ style "transform" (SmoothMoveCSS.transform targetX targetY)
        , style "transition" (SmoothMoveCSS.transitionWithSpeed speed distance defaultConfig)
        ]
        [ text "Speed-based animated element" ]

name: SmoothMovePorts

unions.name: Timing
unions.comment:
Animation timing configuration

Choose between speed-based or duration-based timing:

  - Speed: Animation speed in pixels per second (higher = faster)
  - Duration: Animation duration in milliseconds (higher = slower)

aliases.name: AnimationCommand
aliases.comment:
Animation command data to send to JavaScript

Use this with your own port:

    port animateElement : AnimationCommand -> Cmd msg
aliases.type: { elementId : String.String, targetX : Basics.Float, targetY : Basics.Float, duration : Basics.Float, easing : String.String, axis : String.String }

aliases.name: AnimationSpec
aliases.comment:
Animation specification for batch operations

Use this with `animateBatch` to animate multiple elements at once.
aliases.type: { elementId : String.String, targetX : Basics.Float, targetY : Basics.Float, config : SmoothMovePorts.Config }

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment:
Configuration for port-based animations

  - timing: Animation timing (Speed in pixels per second or Duration in milliseconds)
  - easing: Web Animations API easing ("ease-out", "cubic-bezier(0.4, 0.0, 0.2, 1)", etc.)

aliases.name: PositionUpdate
aliases.comment:
Position update data received from JavaScript

Use this with your own port:

    port positionUpdates : (Decode.Value -> msg) -> Sub msg
aliases.type: { elementId : String.String, x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, isAnimating : Basics.Bool }

values.name: animateBatch
values.type: List.List SmoothMovePorts.AnimationSpec -> SmoothMovePorts.Model -> ( SmoothMovePorts.Model, List.List SmoothMovePorts.AnimationCommand )

values.name: animateBatchWithPort
values.type: (String.String -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg) -> List.List SmoothMovePorts.AnimationSpec -> SmoothMovePorts.Model -> ( SmoothMovePorts.Model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: animateTo
values.comment:
Start animating an element to a target position using default config

Returns the updated model and an animation command for your port.
values.type: String.String -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> SmoothMovePorts.Model -> ( SmoothMovePorts.Model, SmoothMovePorts.AnimationCommand )

values.name: animateToWithConfig
values.comment:
Start animating an element to a target position with custom configuration

Returns the updated model and an animation command for your port.
values.type: SmoothMovePorts.Config -> String.String -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> SmoothMovePorts.Model -> ( SmoothMovePorts.Model, SmoothMovePorts.AnimationCommand )

values.name: animateToX
values.comment:
Start animating an element horizontally to a target X position

Only the X coordinate will change - Y position remains at current value.
Returns the updated model and an animation command for your port.

values.name: animateToXWithConfig
values.comment:
Start animating an element horizontally to a target X position with custom configuration

Only the X coordinate will change - Y position remains at current value.
Returns the updated model and an animation command for your port.
values.type: SmoothMovePorts.Config -> String.String -> Basics.Float -> SmoothMovePorts.Model -> ( SmoothMovePorts.Model, SmoothMovePorts.AnimationCommand )

values.name: animateToY
values.comment:
Start animating an element vertically to a target Y position

Only the Y coordinate will change - X position remains at current value.
Returns the updated model and an animation command for your port.

values.name: animateToYWithConfig
values.comment:
Start animating an element vertically to a target Y position with custom configuration

Only the Y coordinate will change - X position remains at current value.
Returns the updated model and an animation command for your port.
values.type: SmoothMovePorts.Config -> String.String -> Basics.Float -> SmoothMovePorts.Model -> ( SmoothMovePorts.Model, SmoothMovePorts.AnimationCommand )

values.name: defaultConfig
values.comment: Default configuration using Web Animations API

values.name: encodeAnimationCommand
values.comment:
Create a string representation of an animation command for easy port integration

This creates a simple format that's easy to parse in JavaScript:
"elementId:targetX:targetY:duration:easing:axis"

values.name: encodeStopCommand
values.comment: Create a string representation of a stop command

values.name: getAllPositions
values.comment: Get all current element positions
values.type: SmoothMovePorts.Model -> Dict.Dict String.String { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float }

values.name: getPosition
values.comment: Get the current position of a specific element
values.type: String.String -> SmoothMovePorts.Model -> Maybe.Maybe { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float }

values.name: handleAnimationComplete
values.comment: Handle animation completion from JavaScript

values.name: handlePositionUpdate
values.comment:
Handle position updates from JavaScript

Call this from your update function when receiving position updates:

    case Decode.decodeValue positionDecoder value of
        Ok positionUpdate ->
            SmoothMovePorts.handlePositionUpdate positionUpdate model

values.name: handlePositionUpdateFromJson
values.comment:
Handle position updates from JavaScript with automatic JSON decoding

This is the most convenient way to handle position updates - it combines
decoding and model updating in a single function call.

    PositionUpdateMsg value ->
        case SmoothMovePorts.handlePositionUpdateFromJson value model.animations of
            Ok newAnimations ->
                ( { model | animations = newAnimations }, Cmd.none )

            Err _ ->
                ( model, Cmd.none )

values.name: init
values.comment: Initialize empty model

values.name: isAnimating
values.comment: Check if any animations are currently running

values.name: positionUpdateDecoder
values.comment:
Decode position updates from JavaScript

Use this with your port subscription:

    case Decode.decodeValue SmoothMovePorts.positionUpdateDecoder value of
        Ok positionUpdate ->
            SmoothMovePorts.handlePositionUpdate positionUpdate model

values.name: setPosition
values.comment:
Set the position of an element without animation

Call this during initialization to establish element positions, otherwise they will start at (0,0).

Pipe this in your init function for as many elements as needed:

    initialModel =
        SmoothMovePorts.init
            |> SmoothMovePorts.setPosition "element-a" 100 150
            |> SmoothMovePorts.setPosition "element-b" 200 250
            |> SmoothMovePorts.setPosition "element-c" 300 150

values.name: stopAnimation
values.comment:
Stop animation for a specific element

Returns the updated model and the element ID to stop (for your port).

values.name: stopAnimationWithPort
values.comment:
Stop animation for a specific element with automatic port handling

This is the most convenient way to stop a single animation - it handles all the
encoding internally. Just provide your element ID and port function.

    ( newModel, cmd ) =
        stopAnimationWithPort stopElementAnimation "box1" model
values.type: (String.String -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg) -> String.String -> SmoothMovePorts.Model -> ( SmoothMovePorts.Model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: stopBatch
values.comment:
Stop multiple animations at once

Takes a list of element IDs and returns the updated model and list of element IDs that were actually stopped.

    ( newModel, stoppedElements ) =
        stopBatch [ "box1", "box2", "box3", "box4" ] model
values.type: List.List String.String -> SmoothMovePorts.Model -> ( SmoothMovePorts.Model, List.List String.String )

values.name: stopBatchWithPort
values.comment:
Stop multiple animations with automatic port handling

This is the most convenient way to stop multiple animations - it handles all the
encoding and batching internally. Just provide your element IDs and port function.

    ( newModel, cmd ) =
        stopBatchWithPort stopElementAnimation [ "box1", "box2", "box3", "box4" ] model
values.type: (String.String -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg) -> List.List String.String -> SmoothMovePorts.Model -> ( SmoothMovePorts.Model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: transform
values.comment: Create a CSS transform string for positioning

values.name: transformElement
values.comment: Create a CSS transform string by looking up the element's current position

name: SmoothMoveSub

unions.name: Model
unions.comment:
Internal model that manages animation state and element positions automatically

This model handles all animation state AND element positions internally, so developers
don't need to track AnimationState, completion logic, or current positions manually.

Uses a Dict for O(1) lookups and better performance with many elements.

unions.name: Timing
unions.comment:
Animation timing configuration

Choose between speed-based or duration-based timing:

  - Speed: Animation speed in pixels per second (higher = faster)
  - Duration: Animation duration in milliseconds (higher = slower)

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment:
Configuration options for subscription-based animations

  - timing: Animation timing (Speed in pixels per second or Duration in milliseconds)
  - easing: Easing function from elm-community/easing-functions

values.name: animateTo
values.comment:
Start animating an element to a target position using default config

If the element is already animating, it will smoothly transition to the new target.
If the element has no current position, it starts from (0, 0).

    import SmoothMoveSub

    newModel =
        SmoothMoveSub.animateTo "my-element" 200 300 model.smoothMove

values.name: animateToWithConfig
values.comment:
Start animating an element to a target position with custom configuration

    config =
        { defaultConfig | speed = 600.0, easing = Ease.outQuint }

    newModel =
        SmoothMoveSub.animateToWithConfig config "my-element" 100 150 model.smoothMove
values.type: SmoothMoveSub.Config -> String.String -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> SmoothMoveSub.Model -> SmoothMoveSub.Model

values.name: animateToX
values.comment:
Start animating an element horizontally to a target X position

Only the X coordinate will change - Y position remains at current value.

    newModel =
        SmoothMoveSub.animateToX "my-element" 200 model.smoothMove

values.name: animateToXWithConfig
values.comment:
Start animating an element horizontally to a target X position with custom configuration

Only the X coordinate will change - Y position remains at current value.

    config =
        { defaultConfig | timing = Speed 600.0, easing = Ease.outQuint }

    newModel =
        SmoothMoveSub.animateToXWithConfig config "my-element" 200 model.smoothMove

values.name: animateToY
values.comment:
Start animating an element vertically to a target Y position

Only the Y coordinate will change - X position remains at current value.

    newModel =
        SmoothMoveSub.animateToY "my-element" 300 model.smoothMove

values.name: animateToYWithConfig
values.comment:
Start animating an element vertically to a target Y position with custom configuration

Only the Y coordinate will change - X position remains at current value.

    config =
        { defaultConfig | timing = Speed 600.0, easing = Ease.outQuint }

    newModel =
        SmoothMoveSub.animateToYWithConfig config "my-element" 300 model.smoothMove

values.name: defaultConfig
values.comment:
The default configuration which can be modified import Ease
import SmoothMoveSub exposing (defaultConfig)

    defaultConfig : Config
    defaultConfig =
        { speed = 200
        , easing = Ease.outQuint
        , axis = Both
        }

values.name: getAllPositions
values.comment:
Get all current element positions

Returns a dictionary mapping element IDs to their current positions.

    positions =
        SmoothMoveSub.getAllPositions model.smoothMove
values.type: SmoothMoveSub.Model -> Dict.Dict String.String { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float }

values.name: getPosition
values.comment:
Get the current position of a specific element

Returns Nothing if the element has never been animated.

    case SmoothMoveSub.getPosition "my-element" model.smoothMove of
        Just { x, y } ->
            div [ style "transform" (SmoothMoveSub.transform x y) ] [ text "Element" ]

        Nothing ->
            text "Element not found"
values.type: String.String -> SmoothMoveSub.Model -> Maybe.Maybe { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float }

values.name: init
values.comment:
Initialize the model with no active animations

    init =
        SmoothMoveSub.init

values.name: isAnimating
values.comment:
Check if the model is animating

    if SmoothMoveSub.isAnimating model.smoothMove then
        text "Animation running"

    else
        text "No animation"

values.name: setPosition
values.comment:
Set the initial position of an element without animation

This is useful for preventing the "jump to (0,0)" behavior on first animation.
Call this during initialization to establish element positions.

    initialModel =
        SmoothMoveSub.init
            |> SmoothMoveSub.setPosition "element-a" 100 150
            |> SmoothMoveSub.setPosition "element-b" 200 250

values.name: step
values.comment:
Step animations forward by the given time delta (in milliseconds)

Call this function on each animation frame with the time delta.
This function handles all active animations simultaneously and updates element positions.

    import SmoothMoveSub

    type Msg
        = AnimationFrame Float
        | StartMove Float Float

    update msg model =
        case msg of
            AnimationFrame deltaMs ->
                let
                    newSmoothMove =
                        SmoothMoveSub.step deltaMs model.smoothMove
                in
                ( { model | smoothMove = newSmoothMove }
                , Cmd.none
                )

values.name: stopAnimation
values.comment:
Stop animation for a specific element

The element will remain at its current position.

    newModel =
        SmoothMoveSub.stopAnimation "my-element" currentModel.smoothMove

values.name: subscriptions
values.comment:
Simplified subscription function that handles animation logic internally

This function takes care of all the animation frame updates and state management.
You just need to handle the Position updates in your model. The library will
automatically stop sending updates when the animation is complete.

    import SmoothMoveSub exposing (subscriptions)

    type Msg
        = StartMove Float Float
        | AnimationFrame Float
        | NoOp

    subscriptions : Model -> Sub Msg
    subscriptions model =
        SmoothMoveSub.subscriptions AnimationFrame model.smoothMove

    update msg model =
        case msg of
            StartMove targetX targetY ->
                let
                    newSmoothMove =
                        startAnimationTo "element-id" targetX targetY model.smoothMove
                in
                ( { model | smoothMove = newSmoothMove }, Cmd.none )

            AnimationFrame deltaMs ->
                let
                    newSmoothMove =
                        SmoothMoveSub.update deltaMs model.smoothMove
                in
                ( { model | smoothMove = newSmoothMove }
                , Cmd.none
                )

values.name: transform
values.comment:
Create a CSS transform string for positioning

    div [ style "transform" (SmoothMoveSub.transform 100 200) ] [ text "Moving element" ]

values.name: transformElement
values.comment:
Create a CSS transform string by looking up the element's current position

This convenience function eliminates the need to manually call getPosition and handle Maybe values.
If the element is not found, it defaults to (0, 0).

    div [ style "transform" (SmoothMoveSub.transformElement "my-element" model) ] [ text "Moving element" ]
